steps.title: Find the gesture
steps.description: Lightly sketch a single curving line from the top of the head down through the spine to the floor — this is the cat's overall posture. A sitting cat has a gentle backward-leaning curve.

steps.title: Block the silhouette
steps.description: Add a circle for the head and a wider, taller egg shape below it for the body. The body should be about twice the size of the head.

steps.title: Add legs and tail
steps.description: Sketch two front legs going straight down from the body to the ground, and a tail curling around to one side. The back legs are mostly tucked under — just suggest them with a curve at the base.

steps.title: Refine the outline
steps.description: Trace the full silhouette in one confident pass. Add two triangular ears on top of the head.

steps.title: Add facial features
steps.description: Place the eyes roughly halfway down the head, then add a small triangular nose and a curved mouth below it. Keep them centered around the head's vertical axis.

steps.title: Suggest the fur
steps.description: Add a few short strokes at the cheeks, chest, and tail tip to suggest fur. Resist the urge to draw every hair — a few well-placed marks read as fur.
